entry.title: Re: 自動付款可否跨公司不同公司幣別進行付款
entry.content:
Hi OwenLiu,
如圖片所示的敘述，系統自動付款流程如果付款方A公司傳送方B公司，系統限制AB兩公司必須為同一國家同一幣別，
但真實情況是AB兩公司必須是不同國家不同幣別，該如何使用大批付款？

entry.title: Re: How to use side effects in RAP
entry.content:
Hi @Andre_Fischer 

 Thank you for such a good posting, very nice feature.

 We created V2 service by RAP, in the behavior definition we enabled Side-Effects:

  side effects {
    field  BatchNumber            affects entity _Batch;
    action AssignBatchAlternative affects entity _Batch;
  }

 And we see the metadata generated:

<Annotations Target="cds_xctcoxui_purchaseorderitems.xCTCOxC_PurchaseOrderItemsTPType" >
    <Annotation Term="Common.SideEffects" Qualifier="SAP__Field_BatchNumber">
        <Record Type="Common.SideEffectsType">
            <PropertyValue Property="SourceProperties">
                <Collection>
                    <PropertyPath>BatchNumber</PropertyPath>
                </Collection>
            </PropertyValue>
            <PropertyValue Property="TargetEntities">
                <Collection>
                    <NavigationPropertyPath>to_Batch</NavigationPropertyPath>
                </Collection>
            </PropertyValue>
        </Record>
    </Annotation>
</Annotations>
<Annotations Target="cds_xctcoxui_purchaseorderitems.cds_xctcoxui_purchaseorderitems_Entities/AssignBatchAlternative" >
    <Annotation Term="Common.SideEffects" Qualifier="SAP__Action_AssignBatchAlternative">
        <Record Type="Common.SideEffectsType">
            <PropertyValue Property="TargetEntities">
                <Collection>
                    <NavigationPropertyPath>_it/to_Batch</NavigationPropertyPath>
                </Collection>
            </PropertyValue>
        </Record>
    </Annotation>
</Annotations>

We expect the associated entity 'to_Batch' to be refreshed automatically by action 'AssignBatchAlternative' or attribute change 'BatchNumber', but none of these happens.

Can you please suggest how to debug?

SAP S/4HANA 2023

SAP_GWFND 758

UIS4HOP1 900

@chunyang_xu 

Regards

Sensen

entry.title: I need support setting up SAP notifications related to SAP Ariba certificate renewals
entry.content:
I need support setting up SAP notifications related to SAP Ariba certificate renewals so I can notify our IT department in a timely manner.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: I need support setting up SAP notifications related t...
entry.content:
To receive SAP notifications related to SAP Ariba Certificate renewals, an S-user can subscribe to cloud notifications by enabling "Customer Communication" with one of the following authorizations (see KBA 2587408)

- Display System Data
- Edit System Data  

For detailed information, please refer to 2900069 - How S-users manage their own Cloud Notification subscriptions - SAP for Me

Best regards,
Judy

entry.title: Re: Inbound delivery released after packing not appearing...
entry.content: Hi @ Indrajit3 - Could you check the same product's delivery is available in EWM with or without packing where message is showing delivery is distributed ? Please provide screen shots with messages while creation so someone could help... Kind Regards Ajit

entry.title: Re: \/SCWM/TO_CREATE OVER PICKING
entry.content:
Hi @daumsolomon 

"However, OVER PICKING sometimes occurs." - Where business looking to fix at the time of "pick WT creation" and Pick WT confirmation ? I saw in code you used exception code CHHU 

"Pick WT confirmation" - Use BADI to control as per your desired BADI-/SCWM/EX_CORE_CO_CHECK_CONF- Validation while conformation, if overpick then error otherwise good .

Could you help to answer please.

Kind regards,

Ajit

entry.title: Work zone differences and widgets
entry.content:
Hi, sorry. How can I see the templates for de SAP Bussiness Workzone and the widgets that I can use?

Also, what is the difference between SAP Build Work zone and SAP SuccessFactors Work Zone?

Thanks a lot!

entry.title: Re: Can the Inventory valuation for sales order stock be...
entry.content: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition does not support using the same moving average price for sales order stock and normal inventory stock simultaneously. The system handles these two stock types differently: Normal inventory stock uses the moving average price method, which calculates a new material price after each receipt by dividing the total stock value by the total quantity in stock . For sales order stock, SAP recommends not using the moving average price. Instead, the standard price method is preferred for internally manufactured materials . When using valuated sales order stock, the inventory values are updated when goods receipts are entered for internally manufactured or externally procured materials . For sales order stock managed on a quantity and value basis: The standard price is updated by the first goods receipt posting The moving price may change as a result of goods movements and invoices, and can be updated statistically The system calculates the moving average price for sales order stock automatically by dividing the material value in the material stock account by the total of all warehouse inventories in a plant. It's important to note that while both stock types can use moving average prices, they are calculated and managed separately. The public cloud version does not provide a built-in feature to use the same moving average price across both stock types simultaneously. please check https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/idea/338568 and vote to influence the roadmap

entry.title: SAP GUI for HTML: Forward navigation opening new browser tab
entry.content:
Hey guys,

Is there a setting in SAP GUI for HTML, where I can define, that a new tab is opened during forward navigation instead of navigating withing the current browser window?

Thank you for any hint!

Best regards

Tobias

entry.title: Re: ✨ How to Identify SAP Fiori Apps Based on RAP (RESTful Application Programming Model)
entry.content:
Sorry, but this is just wrong. 

You're favourite LLM spit a good sounding article and you copy/pasted it. 

RAP creates APIs which Fiori/UI5 Apps can interact with to show and update date.

It also includes metadata to create a Fiori Elements app with. It's does not need to be FE and OData V4.

If you have ADT just search for the service new and you see the RAP structure. 

I've I see the service definition why should I search further? This "post" just does not make sense.

entry.title: Enabling checkbox Delivery Completed for released Purchase Order
entry.content:
Hi experts,

My customer want the Delivery Completed checkbox to be enable for edit on ME22N or ME29N, even though the Purchase Order document is already Released with no changes. There is no option to unrelease the PO doc on ME29N first then change the Deliv. Compl. because it has been locked so user cannot unrelease the PO doc without certain condition. My functional staff says that this requirement is possible to do because her previous company implemented it, but she didn't know the detail on how they implemented it.

I have checked the local variable of ME_PROCESS_PO_CUST~FIELDSELECTION_ITEM and found the CH_FIELDSELECTION table but there is only single line available which is metafield 9000100 (I have tried to enabled this but its not Deliv. Compl. cb).

I tried to change the SCREEN field value from BAdI ME_PROCESS_PO_CUST~FIELDSELECTION_ITEM, but it still won't work. Here's my code:

METHOD if_ex_me_process_po_cust~fieldselection_item.
  DATA : ls_item   TYPE mepoitem,
         ls_header TYPE mepoheader.

  ls_item   = im_item->get_data( ).
  ls_header = im_header->get_data( ).

  IF sy-tcode = 'ME29N' AND ls_header-frgke = 'R'.
    LOOP AT SCREEN.
      IF screen-name = 'MEPO1313-ELIKZ'.
        screen-required        = 0.
        screen-input           = 1.
        screen-output          = 1.
        screen-intensified     = 0.
        screen-invisible       = 0.
        screen-length          = 0.
        screen-active          = 1.
        screen-display_3d      = 0.
        screen-value_help      = 0.
        screen-request         = 0.
        screen-values_in_combo = 0.
        screen-color           = 0.
        MODIFY SCREEN.
      ENDIF.
    ENDLOOP.
  ENDIF.
ENDMETHOD.

Can anyone pointed out what did I do wrong or any other method I could use? The requirement is only to enable editing the Delivery Completed cb so users can update the delivery status.

I would be really grateful if anyone can help.

Hope you have a great week!

entry.title: SAP RAP Events local Consumption
entry.content:
Hi All,

I am seeing a weird issue with local event consumption of standard RAP events. So, I have seen if we create Purchase order from ME21N SAP GUI, standard RAP BO event Created of R_PurchaseOrderTP is triggered. Once, I implemented the local handler for this entity event, it doesn't trigger immediately. But once I go inside debugging mode, it starts triggering. 

 

Have anyone else faced this issue? Do we have to activate or run any program for this?

entry.title: Re: Service not generated under SICF path /sap/bc/ui5_ui5/sap/ - UI5 app deployment

entry.title: Re: Coupa API for Image scan, PDF read failing to convert...
entry.content:
Hi

I guess you have also tried the API call using Postman (or similar). Can you share the header parameter you have send in your request (don't forget to mask out Authorization, Token, etc. - those are not interesting for the study) and also what you get back as response? In the request there is interesting "Accept" or "Accept-Content" and in the response "Content-Type". Maybe you can also share a part of the response payload. Interesting is the start and the end of the payload - the middle you can also mask out to not share information that are not for everybody 🙂

This might help to give further ideas to you.

BR Helmut

entry.title: How Partner get help from SAP Partner Support Portal
entry.content:
Dear CIC,

 

I am a new joiner at company* and was given a new S-user with access to the Partner Portal. I have assigned the new S-user to my existing SAP Universal ID; however, I am unable to log in to the portal using my new S-user. The error message states that there was a problem with my user. 

I have already started working on a customer engagement project and need access to the Partner Portal resources urgently.

Please check and let me know how to resolve the issue.

 

Thanks,

 

PERSON_NAME


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How Partner get help from SAP Partner Support Portal
entry.content:
For detail please check in KBA1848459 - How do SAP Partners get help with all partner topics and applications (including Partner Dashboards in SAP for Me) - SAP for Me

Partners must contact SAP Partner Support directly for all partner topics.
IMPORTANT: DO NOT create a support case in SAP for Me for any of these partner topics since the online support team cannot assist with these partner topics:

  • All Partner applications (including Partner Dashboards and Partner applications in SAP for Me)
    • Partner Solutions dashboard https://me.sap.com/partner/dashboard/partnerSolutions
    • Partnership dashboard https://me.sap.com/partner/dashboard/partnership
    • SAP Customer Success applications
    • Sales and Marketing applications, etc…..
    • SAP Partner Portal https://partneredge.sap.com (including login issues)
    • SAP Partner Pricing application https://partnersappartnerpricingapp.cfapps.eu10.hana.ondemand.com/index.html
  • Partner Authorizations for S-user IDs (includes all partner authorizations in SAP for Me and/or SAP Partner Portal)
  • Partner Roles (PRM_CP and other PRM* roles) which are assigned to S-users in the SAP Partner Portal (https://partneredge.sap.com)
  • Partner Training and Certifications
  • All other Partner questions and processes (e.g. How to questions specific to partners)

Who to Contact

  • Contact the SAP Partner Support by using the "Get Support" on the partner "Get assistance" dashboard.
  • For the most current contact options for SAP Partner Support and the helpline, go to http://partneredge.sap.com and select 'Get Support' or visit https://partneredge.sap.com/en/public/get-help.html 
  • Contact your Partner Experience Manager which is your dedicated SAP Contact assigned to each partner.
    Note: If you don't know who your Partner Experience Manager is, contact SAP Partner Support using the "Get Support" on the partner "Get assistance" dashboard (screenshots below).

entry.title: Retrieve historical compensation items from Employee Central to complete Compensation module workshe
entry.content:
Hello dear,

I'd like to share with you a precise expression of need and my difficulties to do it :

(1) The objective is twofold:
- recover as much compensation and job information as possible from Employee Central to feed the worksheet design of a compensation campaign, person by person, for each year,
- to limit the use of LookUp tables as much as possible, unless no other solution can be found.

(2) For most “campaign year” information, it's simple
- I'll take the preparation of the 2025 compensation campaign as an example
- simply indicate in the worksheet design Employee Central field
- the value for the current year will be retrieved and displayed.
- This works very well for the Base Salary 2025 or the team on the campaign date, for example.

(3) However, this does not work as easily for previous years' compensation data for Base Salary and Bonus N-1, N-2 as described below.

Someone told us that one possible solution was to prepare the data via Integration Center and feed it into the worksheet.

I started by building an MDF object with 5 basic pieces of information:
- User
- Base salary N-1
- Base salary N-2
- Bonus N-1
- Bonus N-2

I want to collect information from Employee Central via an integration to complete the MDF Object.

But when I try to set up an Inbound Integration I face some difficulties such as :
* collecting salary information for two consecutive years in the same integration, how can I retrieve them (ie Base Salary N-1 and Base Salary N-2)?
* how to collect this information also for Non Recurring Pay Components, i.e. the elements that make up the Spot Bonus, and sum them into a single total bonus per person per year (Total Bonus = Incentive + Profit Sharing + Cash Bonus + Variable Bonus, etc.)?

Would you have the possibility to give me some helpful advices on how to do it ?
This type of configuration must have been frequently implemented by many SF users, perhaps a tutorial already exists?

BR, Martin

entry.title: Re: SAPSAC : can we have % values and absolute values tog...
entry.content:
Hello @SACSuresh

As far as I know you cannot have two key figures in this diagram at the same time.

You need to place a chart, like bar chart to display the absolute values.

BR, Martin

entry.title: CMR Creation with Reference: Process Flow in Subsequent Billing Doc Differs with Reference Level
entry.content:
You can create credit memo requests with header reference, item reference, or both. The process flow in subsequent billing documents varies according to the level of reference you use.

  • Item reference, or header and item reference

When you create a credit memo request with item reference, the referenced document will show in the process flow in the subsequent billing document.

Example: An invoice (INV0003130) is referenced on header and item level to create a credit memo request. You can see the invoice in the process flow of the subsequent billing document in the Manage Billing Documents app.

  • Header reference only

When you create a credit memo request with header reference only, the referenced document will not show in the process flow in the subsequent billing document.

Example: An invoice (INV0003129) is referenced only on header level to create a credit memo request. The invoice is not shown in the process flow of the subsequent billing document in the Manage Billing Documents app.

Note: Although not shown in the process flow (in the Manage Billing Documents app), the preceding invoice (INV0003129) can show in the document flow (in the Display Billing Documents and Change Billing Documents apps). This is because the document flow always exposes the entire related document chain, while the process flow offers a simplified representation of the document chain that focuses on documents central to the responsibilities of the billing clerk.

entry.title: Re: SAP BDC Demo Environment
entry.content: I guess this one: https://www.sap.com/products/data-cloud/trial.html

entry.title: Re: Restricted Access to Pricing Configuration Elements i...
entry.content: For 'Set Sales Pricing Procedure', you may need to assign BC 'SAP_CA_BC_IC_LND_SD4_PC'. BRs, Archer

entry.title: Re: Assigned G/L account is displayed as "00NOTASSGND"
entry.content:
Hi,

In SAP Financial Statement Versions, "00NOTASSGND" (Not Assigned") means that GL account is not assigned to any specific node or item within the financial statement hierarchy.  When a G/L account is not assigned to a particular item within the Financial Statement Version, it's automatically displayed under this "Not Assigned" node. Make sure its assigned.

 

Regards

Vijaykumar

entry.title: Панове, давайте знайомитися!
entry.content:
Товариство, добрий ранок,

Приємно, що ця спільнота активно розвивається і до неї долучаються усе нові учасники.😊

Тому пропоную трішки познайомитися і коротко написати про себе:🤗

- ПІБ

- Місце проживання

- Скільки років у SAP

- У якому модулі/рішенні працюєте

Із вашого дозволу я почну.😎

Мої вітання панство,

Мене звати Бабченко Ярослав. Проживаю у Полтаві. Більше 12 років у SAP. Сертифікований консультант SAP PP.

Бажаю усім миру і активного залучення😉

entry.title: About About purchasing and production
entry.content: As shown in the figure, Company A wants to purchase from a supplier and ship directly to the processing destination. Which scope is applicable in the public cloud version, and how does it make a purchase order?

entry.title: Re: Hi, we have a Classic opex model with legal entity, profit center, cost center and wbs.
entry.content: Hi William, in a Classic sap centric model, opex could be by cost center or wbs. Legal entity and profit center are “derived” / “validated” based on the masterdata. In a layout that Expose cost center as key dimension, legal entity and profit center must be consistendo with the corresponding cost center masterdata attribuite. In a separate layout, wbs is the key dimension and here is the wbs masterdata that guide the consistency of legal entity and profit center. Once planned the data, total opex are then consistent agenda looking legal entity or/and profit center.

entry.title: Troubleshooting Guide for Known Issues in SAP Green Ledger Analytical Dashboard
entry.content:
Introduction

In this blog, I’ll walk you through six common issues encountered in SAP Green Ledger Analytical Dashboard along with step-by-step solutions.

 

Applicable Versions

This troubleshooting guide applies to specific versions only. The issues described in this article are known and will be resolved in the upcoming version. Please stay tuned for the next release.

SAP Analytics Cloud Versions: 1.0.0 and 1.1.0

SAP Datasphere Versions: 1.0.0 and 1.1.1

 

Issue 1 Story Management Error

Symptom

There are alerts marked with exclamation mark icons in the drop-down menu of Add New Data from the toolbar in SAP Analytics Cloud.

Procedure

  1. Open SAP Green Ledger Analytical Dashboard in SAP Analytics Cloud.
  2. Open edit mode.
  3. From the toolbar, select Add New Data > [The Model to be Replaced] > Replace....
  4. If a warning pops up, choose Continue.
  5. In the Select Model dialog, select Select other model... > SAPDWC in the Datasphere section > [Your Workspace Name] > [Model Name] (should match the name of the model to be replaced).
  6. Choose OK.
  7. In the Replace Model panel, choose Preview > Replace Model. For more information, see Replace Models in Stories (Optimized) | SAP Help Portal.
  8. From the toolbar, select Add New Data > [The Model to be Replaced] > Remove to remove the replaced model.
  9. Repeat steps 3 to 8 until all analytic models are replaced.

 

Issue 2 Incorrect CO2e-Flow Statement

Applicable Version

SAP Datasphere Applicable Version: 1.0.0

This issue doesn't occur in the latest version. We recommend upgrading your system to the latest version to resolve the problem.

Symptom

The opening and closing data of CO2e flow statement in SAP Green Ledger Analytical Dashboard isn't correct.

For more information about SAP Green Ledger Analytical Dashboard, see SAP Green Ledger Overview | SAP Help Portal.

Procedure

  1. Download the ZIP file here.
  2. Extract the ZIP file.
  3. In SAP Datasphere, open the Repository Explorer.
  4. Above the result table, choose Import > Import Objects from CSN/JSON File.
  5. Select a space that you want to import.
  6. Select and import the JSON file you just downloaded.
  7. Select all objects with status Ready to Import. Exclude objects marked as Already in the Repository.
  8. Choose Import CSN File.
  9. Once finished, select the imported objects and choose Deploy.
  10. In the Repository Explorer,  search for the business name "Co2e Flow Statement Union (HL)" and open it.
  11. In line 28, replace "OpenTranToCustomers" with "FiscalYearPeriod".
  12. In lines 33 and 66, replace "SAP_GRL_HL_EmissionSumTranToCustomers" with "SAP_GRL_HL_EmissionSumTranToCustomersWOP".
  13. In line 61, replace "CloseTranToCustomers" with "FiscalYearPeriod".
  14. Choose Save and Deploy.

 

Issue 3 Currency or Unit Conversion Error

Symptom

A currency/unit conversion error message occurs while previewing the green ledger analytic model in SAP Datasphere.

Procedure

In SAP Datasphere, search for the technical name "SAP_GRL_HL_JrnlEntrEmissionLdngLdgrUntCnvrsn" in the Repository Explorer.

  • If no results are found, perform the following steps.
    1. Download the ZIP file here.
    2. Extract the ZIP file.
    3. Choose Import > Import Objects from CSN/JSON File.
    4. Select and import the JSON file you just downloaded to space "SAP_CONTENT".
    5. Select all objects with status Ready to Import. Exclude objects marked as Already in the Repository.
    6. Choose Import CSN File.
    7. Once finished, select the imported objects and choose Deploy.
  • If results are found, perform the following steps.
    1. Open the view "SAP_GRL_HL_JrnlEntrEmissionLdngLdgrUntCnvrsn".
    2. Choose Details in the top right corner > fx icon in the data flow > Emission Amount in Columns.
    3. Under the Expression field, locate the line "SCHEMA" => 'SAP_CONTENT'.
    4. Replace the "SAP_CONTENT" to your space name.
    5. Choose Save and Deploy.

 

Issue 4 Duplicate Data Caused by Unsupported Locale Name

Symptom

Duplicate data can occur when an unsupported language locale name is used in SAP Analytics Cloud.

The supported locale for English in SAP Analytics Cloud is "en". However, the data imported from SAP Green Ledger uses the locale "en_US_sapigi", which results in duplicate data.

Note: If you're using a non-English system, this issue won't affect your data output. However, it’s still recommended to perform the following steps to prevent future issues.

Procedure

  1. In SAP Datasphere, search "SAP_GRL_IL_T_GHGEnergyMix" in the Repository Explorer and open it.
  2. Choose Details in the upper right corner.
  3. In the Model Properties screen, choose Edit Columns next to Attributes. 
  4. Change the string length to 14 in the Language field.
  5. Choose Close.
  6. Choose Save and Deploy.
  7. From the following list, search one of technical names in the Repository Explorer.
    • SAP_GRL_IL_T_ModeOfTransport
    • SAP_GRL_IL_T_GHGEnergySourcingType
    • SAP_GRL_IL_T_GHGEnergyClassification
    • SAP_GRL_IL_T_GHGDataQualityCharacteristic
    • SAP_GRL_IL_T_GHGCategory
    • SAP_GRL_IL_T_GHGScope
  8. Choose Details in the top right corner > fx icon in the data flow > Language in Columns.
  9. Change the numbers in Length and Expression fields to 14 as shown in the following screen shot.

  10. Choose Save and Deploy.
  11. Repeat the steps 7 to 10 until all six objects are edited.

 

Issue 5 Filter Unavailable Due to Missing Hierarchy in the Data Source

Symptom

The filters in SAP Green Ledger Analytical Dashboard are unavailable due to missing hierarchy.

Procedure

  1. Open SAP Green Ledger Analytical Dashboard.
  2. Open the filter menu of the unavailable filter, select Edit Filter.
  3. Choose Flat Presentation hierarchy in the pop-up window.
  4. Choose OK.

 

Issue 6 Business Content Import Fails Due to Time Tables and Dimensions Error

Symptom

Business content import failed in SAP Analytics Cloud. This can be checked in Import Summary. To access Import Summary, see Access Import Summary from Monitor Page.

Procedure

  1. Open SAP Datasphere, in the side navigation area, choose Space Management, locate your space tile, and choose Edit to open it.
  2. In the Time Data section, choose Create Time Tables and Dimensions to open the dialog.
  3. Keep the default values unchanged.
  4. Choose Create. Please see Create Times Dimension or use the In-App Help directly in SAP Datasphere for more information.

 

Related Information

SAP Green Ledger Analytical Dashboard | SAP Help Portal

Carbon Data Dashboard | SAP Help Portal

entry.title: Re: Need information on the Composable Storefront
entry.content:
Hi there, 

Apart from the SAP Help documentation, you may also check out the vast amount of learning resources on SAP Learning - learning.sap.com.

You will find quick videos, courses and live sessions at https://learning.sap.com/search?access=free&access=subscription&access=partner&access=employee&page=1&query=spartacus

Hope that helps 🙂

entry.title: Who to contact for SAP Partner CoE Quality Measurement Reporting application (PCOE)
entry.content:
hello support

Please, we have a major problem for the  location. None of the S-users created in SAP for ME of location  have access to the "Service Report (Partner)" authorization. This permission is required to view the Partner PERSON_NAME Quality Reporting for this location. Even the superuser for this location doesn't have access to this report. Can you please help us? We have an emergency due to the new PCOE certification method that is currently being implemented.


regards,


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Who to contact for SAP Partner CoE Quality Measuremen...
entry.content:
KBA 3198857 - Who to contact for SAP Partner CoE Quality Measurement Reporting application (PCOE) - SAP Partner - SAP for Me

If partner S-users need assistance with the SAP Partner CoE Quality Measurement Reporting application (PCOE), they must create a case under component SV-BO-REQ-PCOE.

  • If the user is not able to create a case, they may need to email the PCOE audit team sap_pcoe_audit@sap.com, contact the SAP Partner Support using the "Get Support" on the partner Get assistance dashboard., or use the "Contact Us" in the SAP Partner Portal.
  • Please DO NOT create a case under XX-SER-FORME since they only support SAP for Me applications starting with https://me.sap.com*.

If partner S-users require partner PCOE authorization (Service Reports (Partner)), they must contact their company's Partner Service Manager (S-user ID) who is their dedicated SAP contact for managing S-users/authorizations their partner account. If you're not sure who that is, contact the SAP Partner Support via the partner Get assistance dashboard.

entry.title: About purchasing and production
entry.content:
As shown in the figure,

The purchase requisition in ① is not created manually, but based on MRP.If want to ship directly to the processing destination with that purchase requisition document, how should I set it up and operate it?⇒Please also introduce the purchasing information data.

entry.title: Selective Data Transition with Time Slice.
entry.content:
Selective Data Transition with Time Slice

This post is regarding the Selective Data Transition with time slice.

What is Time Slice?

Applying a time slice allows you to selectively exclude data before a defined fiscal year from the scope of your digital blueprint.

Benefits

Source systems often contain a long history of data, which can lead to long migration runtimes and a high number of errors or inconsistencies in the post-processing phase (for example SFIN conversion). In many cases, this historical data is no longer necessary for operational, reporting, or auditing purposes.

The Lean Selective Data Transition SAP ECC to SAP S/4HANA scenario provided with SAP Business Transformation Center allows you to selectively exclude data related to specific organizational units (company codes) from your migration project. The time slice solution pattern offers the additional option of excluding data before a certain date, which reduces the system size even further.

This data reduction often means you can avoid additional memory extensions and reduce the risks associated with bringing along old and irrelevant data. It not only simplifies the migration process but also ensures a more relevant and valuable dataset at the end of the migration.

The time slice solution pattern uses a specific key date that determines which data is migrated and which is left behind. Transactional data created before the key date is not migrated, unless it is still in open status on the key date. All master data is migrated independently of the time slice, and transactional data created after the key date is also migrated.

The key date is always the start date of a fiscal year. The fiscal year can be different from the calendar year and is associated with the fiscal year variant.

To apply a time slice, you just need to select the fiscal year after which data should be included in the migration. The key date is the first day of the selected fiscal year, as derived from the fiscal year variant(s) of all the company codes that are set as In Scope for the digital blueprint. The time slice solution pattern is applied to all company codes in the scope of the digital blueprint.

After applying a time slice, the time slice-related filters are applied to all supported transformation objects in the modeling phase.

More specifically, the following data is migrated:

  • All master data is migrated by default, even master data that's time-dependent and is dated before the key date of the time slice.
  • Transactional data that is dated after the key date of the time slice.
  • Transactional data that is in open status on the key date of the time slice. This could be, for example, transactional data from a Financial Accounting (FI) document with a posting date before the time slice key date, but with a clearing date that is after the time slice key date.

All transactional data related to standard transformation objects that come from SAP applications aren't within the scope of the time slice solution pattern. Time slice is supported for FI (Financial Accounting) and CO (Controlling) applications. Transactional data that doesn't come from FI and CO applications is migrated, independently of the time slice. The same is the case for all custom transformation objects.

Once a time slice has been defined in the scoping phase, the time slice filtering is applied to the transformation objects that are in scope in one of the following ways:

  • No time slice is applied: The time slice is not applied to these transformation objects because they belong to an SAP component that is not supported by the time slice solution pattern. Currently, only standard transformation objects coming from FI (Financial Accounting) and CO (Controlling) components are supported.
  • Filtering via a predecessor transformation object: These transformation objects are related to predecessors that already have a time slice applied. Therefore, a separate time slice filter is not needed.
  • Filtering via a single table field: The time slice for these transformation objects is based on a single filter criterion. This can either be a specific date or the fiscal year. All data on or after the key date or fiscal year will be selected.

  •  

  • Filtering using multiple filtering criteria: A time slice for these transformation objects is based on several filter groups, each of which consists of certain filtering criteria.

Things to remember: - Only one date can be considered for the time slice key date. If the fiscal year start date is not identical for all company codes that are in scope, then the earliest calendar date is considered as the time slice key date. This means that, for some company codes, there could be more data included in the migration.

For example, let's say you have some company codes in scope with a fiscal year start date of January 1st and other company codes with a fiscal year start date of April 1st. If you select 2022 as the fiscal year for the time slice, the key date will be 1.1.2022, as this is the earliest calendar date between the two fiscal year start dates. This means that, for the company code whose 2022 fiscal year starts on 1.4.2022, the data of the last 3 periods of the previous fiscal year (period 10, period 11 and period 12 of fiscal year 2021) will also be migrated.

A time slice needs to include a minimum of two fiscal years. If, for example, you're defining the time slice in mid-2024, the latest possible fiscal year you can select for the time slice is 2022.

For transactional data to be correctly included or excluded from the scope depending on its status, the period end closing needs to have been performed correctly for all fiscal years before the time slice start date.

Constraints

Before applying the time slice solution pattern, be aware of the following constraints:

  • The fiscal year variant that is currently assigned to a company code (table <T001> and field <PERIV>) reflects the correct period definition for all fiscal years. If this is not the case, the key date for the time slice start cannot be determined correctly.

This is even more important when a change of the fiscal year has been applied in the past.

  • Document reversals might not be possible after the migration if the original document wasn’t migrated. For example, it may not be possible to reverse an FI invoice if the corresponding logistics invoice in Material Management (MM) wasn't migrated.
  • CO documents for expense postings before the time slice key date might not be migrated, as the migration of CO documents depends on the object type. Specifically, transactional data before the time slice key date that is related to object numbers starting with KS, KL, AO, and EO is not migrated.

This could mean that CO document line item postings before the time slice key date on a cost center are not migrated, and this can result in having an FI document with expense lines and an account assignment to a CO object (for example, a cost center), but no corresponding CO document.

  • FI balances at the beginning of the time slice cannot be explained by their line items for accounts that are not open item managed. This is because FI documents are migrated with their open items, but the document may also include G/L accounts that are not open item managed. Balance sheet account line items with a posting date before the time slice key date may be migrated, while, on the other hand, the balances on the same G/L accounts before the time slice key date are not migrated.

For example, let's say the time slice key date is 01.01.2021. When you display the balance of a G/L account for the fiscal year 2019, the balance is zero. When you drill down to the line item display, however, there may be some line items present. The reason is that these G/L account line items are included in an FI document with open items.

  • Complete document flows are not guaranteed. For example, the sales order of a document flow might not be migrated if it was created before the time slice key date and has a completed status. However, the related invoice and open item on the customer account are migrated because they are created within the time slice.
  • When using standard reports to compare open items on customer, vendor, or G/L accounts, discrepancies may arise between the source and target systems if the report's key date is earlier than the time slice key date. This occurs because standard reports include items that were open on the report key date (i.e., posted on or before it and cleared after it). However, if an item was both posted and cleared before the time slice key date, it is not migrated when the time slice solution pattern is active. Consequently, this can cause differences between the source and target system reports.

For example, the time slice key date is 01.01.2022. An invoice on a customer account has a posting date of 13.08.2020 and a clearing date of 15.01.2021. When you report open items with a report key date of 01.01.2021, this item will be included in the report from the source system. In the target system, however, this item will not be available and consequently not included in the report. The reason is that this item was not migrated to the target system, as both its posting date and clearing date are before the time slice key date.

entry.title: Error message displayed on return purchase order(Scope Item BMK)
entry.content:
Hi Expert!

I created purchase type ZNB2 by copying purchase type NB2 (Enh.Ret.to Supplier).
When I tried to create a RETURN purchase order with purchase type ZNB2, an error message appeared.

The following settings have been completed:
・Config ID:101926　Customer code assigned to Plant
・Config ID:103424　Delivery type RL assigned to Purchase type ZNB2
・BP master　Flag the supplier as a return supplier.
・BP master　Register shipping data for supplier.

Also, the creation can be done successfully for purchase type NB2.

What setting am I missing?

Soma

entry.title: Re: Does SAP DRC requires any more access point after get...

entry.title: Re: Solmanの構築について
entry.content:
ご担当者　様

平素よりお世話になっております。

新規インストールを行う場合、一般的な推奨として、最新のサポートパッケージである SAP Solution Manager 7.2 SP 20 のインストールをお勧めします。
スタックファイルの作成には、Maintenance Planner を使用し、最新のサポートパッケージを選択することを推奨いたします。
インストール手順につきましては下記のナレッジ技術文章をご参考お願い致します。

2884721 - How to Install the SAP Solution Manager 7.2 with a specific Support Package

バージョン情報につきましては下記のナレッジ技術文章をご参考お願い致します。

2771041 - SAP Solution Manager 7.2: Support Package stack levels

ご不明な点がございましたら、ご遠慮なくお問い合わせください。

宜しくお願い致します。

SAPサポート

entry.title: ABAP_CLOUD_READINESS Custom Fields and Logic
entry.content:
Hi 
I have extended S/4 system by using the "Custom Fields and Logic" app - this works nicely. Without any code at all.

But when I then use the "Custom Code Migration Projects" to analyze the system these new object are in that list - how should I handle that - because I would like to have a clean-code system as possible ?

Please advise

Thanks

Lars

entry.title: Re: 503 erro on chat SAP AI Lauchpad
entry.content: Hi, can you please report an incident to SAP via CA-ML-AILP component.

entry.title: Re: Introducing Analytical Insights in Joule: Empowering Smarter Decisions, Instantly
entry.content:
Hello,

Yes, all customers who have setup Analytical Insights will be able to run queries on top of their acquired data. The roadmap for the support of standalone DSP is still under discussion. There is currently no plan to support live HANA Cloud.

Best wishes,

Flavia

entry.title: MTO with Special Prc Key
entry.content:
I am facing Condition Error, where my material (2240000005) is manufactured (SFG1) in plant 2021 and is transferred to Plant 2001. This transferred material will then be used in the production of second SFG in Plant 2001. This is entire MTO process. 

The issue is when I execute the sales order cost estimate system is not taking the cost of the material using special proc key. I need expert guide on resolving this matter.

@jmadevcosting @SAP_Test_Costing

entry.title: Re: How I Built a Raised Bed with Free Barn Wood
entry.content: Next step: Automatic irrigation system with drought detection. You could build a Fiori UI to get an overview of the dry areas on the bed 😉

entry.title: Re: Altenative To where used list
entry.content:
There's no up-to-date result because the right answer is that the where-used list for standard SAP objects provides "an incomplete result or no result at all" since 6.10 (a long time ago), as explained in the SAP note 401389 - Where-used list in the Workbench as of Release 6.10 - SAP for Me, unless you decide to build the where-used list as explained in the SAP notes (but it's not advised because the job takes one week or more + many gigabytes of disk space). Only the where-used list of custom objects is complete because compilation generates it.

Also, D010TAB doesn't contain the where-used list of tables, it contains the list of DDIC types used by compiled programs, which is used to determine invalidated ABAP loads. D010TAB can be sufficient if all programs used in production have been also used (and are up-to-date) in the current system where D010TAB is queried. The ABAP loads are stored in the table REPOLOAD, those which are up-to-date have UDAT/UTIME >= SDAT/STIME.

entry.title: Re: Error DMC_RT_MSG 41 _IT_IO_S
entry.content:
Hi tay,

The issue might happened due to rule inconsistency.

Kindly check this KBA.

3501656 - Overwrite error: Column at serialization XX actual column 

Best regards,
Latha

entry.title: Re: Delivery 80000057 does not exist in the database or in the archive

entry.title: Re: Budget Upload Issue in S4HANA Public Cloud – Seeking...
entry.content:
Dear @Bharat23,

please make yourself familiar with the Plan Data Scope, the following blog post will help you 
Understanding the Plan Data Scope.

Depending on the combination of your plan data scope settings and the data you uploaded, data will be updated.

Once you define the plan data scope and disjoint your data uploads, you can avoid unintentionally overwriting existing data.

Best regards
Susanne

entry.title: Re: Activating Standard Fiori Applications
entry.content:
In the step 6, the t-code should be: /IWFND/MAINT_SERVICE.

In case of a service of ODATA V4, use this t-code instead: /IWFND/V4_ADMIN

entry.title: We cannot assign separate number ranges to different plants in the subcontracting challan
entry.content:
We have created a number range prefix DCH for subcontracting challans in SAP Cloud, using billing type JSN. We now need to set separate number range for 8 different plants.

Is this possible? If so, could you please suggest the relevant BAdI name?

We have tried using the SD_BIL_FLEX_NUMBERING BAdI, but it is not working. We are using the Create Subcontracting Challan – India application to create subcontracting challans.

entry.title: Re: How I Built a Raised Bed with Free Barn Wood
entry.content:
The intention was to move away from IT, not get closer to it 🙂
But I would be lying if I said I hadn't already thought about something similar. I quickly dismissed the idea because of the effort involved.

entry.title: Re: SAP EWM Podcast - Episode 21 - Maintenance Logistics with SAP EWM
entry.content:
Hi Fernando, 

SAP EWM integrates the recipient location and goods recepient as a simple field on the PDO/outbound delivery order item to EWM. There is no transfer of the address in SAP EWM. 

One option would be to transfer the address of the recipient location as a document address to the outbound delivery. This would then be on the delivery. There are different technical possibilities. During outbound delivery distribution after the trigger for material staging (MB26), you could read the address data in the interface (/SCWM/OUTB_DLV_SAVEREPLICA) from the PMO (for example, at item level) and specifying the delivery item as the "document address". Typically, these would be the following enhancement options:
• ECC BAdI: SMOD_V50B0001. method: EXIT_SAPLV50I_003
• EWM: BAdI: /SCWM/EX_ERP_MAPIN_OD_SAVEREPL

Feel free to try it out.
BR
Andreas

entry.title: Re: SAP Dynamic Forms 1.0 Released Features
entry.content:
Hi 

Is the form and its data entered stored in BTP or converted to PDF and stored in SAP backend ?

entry.title: Re: Minimum Sales-Based Rent Settlement
entry.content:
Hi, the sequence normally is RERAPP first and then execute RESRSE.

I tested this internally using reference condition types 60 - SBR and 61 - Minimum SBR. Make sure that both condition types are assigned to you sales rule by checking the sub-tab "Terms" on the Condition Types tab.

When running RESRSE the system takes the Minium SBR of 3000 into consideration and does not add another 1000 from the SBR calculation. 

If you are not using the standard condition types, check your configuration against the standard condition types to make sure they are classified correctly as SBR condition types.

Regards, Andre

entry.title: Re: MTO with Special Prc Key
entry.content:
ProcessTransaction Code

Create Production Order

CO01

Goods Issue for Production

MB1A (Movement Type 261)

Goods Receipt for Production

MB31 (Movement Type 101)

Stock Transfer (One-Step)

MB1B (Movement Type 301)

Stock Transfer (Two-Step)

MB1B (Movement Type 303 & 305)

Create Stock Transport Order

ME21N

Goods Receipt for STO

MIGO

entry.title: Re: Purchase Order upload from Legacy to SAP with purchase order history
entry.content:
Hi,

Could you send me sample upload file to load PO history?  Thank You !

sravanj.05@gmail.com

Thanks

entry.title: Re: About About purchasing and production
entry.content:
Hello dear user,

And thank you for asking your question in the SAP Community blog.

Based on the diagram, Company A wants to order something from a supplier but have it shipped directly to a processing location not to their own warehouse. This kind of setup is actually quite common and can be handled in a couple of ways in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, depending on the exact process.

 

If the supplier is delivering directly to a subcontractor (who will perform some processing or manufacturing), then the relevant process is called subcontracting.

In that case, the right scope item is:

3B5 – Subcontracting (External Processing)

Here's how it works:

  • Company A creates a purchase order for the service (e.g., assembly, coating).
  • SAP knows that materials (components) will be sent to the subcontractor.
  • Once the work is done, the subcontractor ships the finished goods either to Company A or directly to the customer (depending on the setup).

 

If the supplier is shipping finished goods directly to another third party (e.g. a customer or an external plant), and Company A just wants to manage the order, this is called third-party processing.

In that case, the relevant scope item is:

2E9 – Third-Party Processing (Sales)

Here:

  • Company A doesn’t physically receive the goods.
  • The supplier ships directly to the end destination.
  • Company A still manages the purchase and the billing.

 

So, which one applies here?

From the labels in the image (including the Japanese text mentioning “external processing”), it looks like this is a subcontracting case so scope item 3B5 is probably what you need.

 

I hope this will help.

Best regards,

Jeremy

entry.title: Re: MTO with Special Prc Key

Best regards

entry.title: Re: Rule Context for Onboarding 2.0 is not set to No
entry.content: Under If Condition 1. Select Lookup (Process). 2. Select Ready to Hire = Yes 3. in Where select Process Type = Onboarding.

entry.title: Re: CPI Connection Error

entry.title: Re: import outbound delivery through the Excel
entry.content:
Hi @omega-ou ,

According to my knowledge, uploading from Excel file is not possible. However, you can try to use apps "My Schedule Lines - Due for Delivery" or "My Sales Orders - Due for Delivery". They offer more flexibility than the app that you are currently using. 

Best regards

entry.title: Re: Goods receipt not possible for delivery 80000391: err...
entry.content: hello, choose the right Confirmation control key in the Confirmation tab.

entry.title: Re: APIs Billing Document can create the Withholding tax...
entry.content:
Hello dear user,

And thank you for asking your question in the SAP Community blog.

You're using the "Billing Document – Create from External System" API to create billing documents in SAP which is a good choice !

If your third-party system has withholding tax info, you're probably wondering if you can send that directly through the API.

The short answer is :

No, the standard billing document API doesn’t allow you to directly pass withholding tax data in the payload.

Why?

Because SAP usually handles withholding tax automatically based on the customer master data (like withholding tax type/code) and your system configuration. So, instead of manually sending the withholding tax from the third-party system, SAP expects to calculate it on its own when the billing document is created.

 

I hope this will help.

Best regards,

Jeremy

entry.title: Re: Need to disable multiple users
entry.content:
Hm, I still think Mark's suggestion to use a login procedure to prevent the respective users (however you can identify those) from login would work...

As to the suggested "DROP USER" statement. Please note the relevant warning:

Dropping a user also deletes all database objects (such as tables or procedures) that they own, as well as any external logins for the user. In addition, if the user is specified in the USER clause of any services, then those services are also dropped.

This may or may not be a problem in your case. I do surely know I have dropped users (from test databases) and was surprised that objects were silently gone, I certainly had expected the engine would warn about existing objects (as a kind of FOREIGN KEY ON DELETE RESTRICED behaviour...).

entry.title: Re: Debugging custom logic using ADT in SAP S/4Hana Cloud
entry.content: So the company I'm at has now upgraded to a 3 tier environment - so debugging is now possible - to a certain extent.   Is there any way of setting an External Breakpoint like we could do in the old days ?   We have custom logic BAdIs implemented for WorkFlows that presumably run under SAP_WFRT not the user that triggers the workflow.

entry.title: Re: the APP mass change of sales order can't set delivery...
entry.content: @Stanislaw_Stefanowski,the APP is mass change of sales order.

entry.title: Re: Assigned G/L account is displayed as "00NOTASSGND"
entry.content:
Hello dear user,

And thank you for asking your question in the SAP Community blog.

When you see "00NOTASSGND" in the Journal Entry Analyzer app, it usually means that a G/L account used in a journal entry hasn’t been assigned to any group in your Financial Statement Version.

In other words, the system doesn’t know where to place it in the financial hierarchy, so it throws it into a default "not assigned" bucket.

 

In the Manage Global Hierarchies app (where you maintain the FSV), every G/L account should be linked to a specific node like "Assets", "Expenses", etc.

If a G/L account is missing from that structure, the system can’t categorize it properly and labels it as 00NOTASSGND.

 

How to fix it:

1. Open the “Manage Global Hierarchies” app
Find your Financial Statement Version (like YCOA or your custom one) and open it for editing.

2. Look for unassigned accounts
Scroll through the hierarchy and check if the G/L account that’s showing as “00NOTASSGND” is included anywhere. If not, that’s the problem.

3. Add the missing G/L account
Choose the appropriate section of the hierarchy (e.g., under "Expenses" or "Revenues") and add the missing G/L account or range of accounts.

4. Save and activate the updated hierarchy.

5. Go back to the Journal Entry Analyzer and check again—your G/L account should now show up in the right place.

 

Here's a quick tip:

If you’re not sure which accounts are unassigned, check what’s appearing under “00NOTASSGND” in the Journal Entry Analyzer and note down those G/L numbers. Then you can go back and assign them in the hierarchy.

 

I hope this will help.

Best regards,

Jeremy

entry.title: Re: Gender Dimension problem
entry.content: Hi @DariusSoreata - Person ID in the Personal Info need to be setup as Secondary Person.  Primary Person is used for Users Sys ID.

entry.title: Functional Area Hierarchy in Group Financial Statements Review booklet
entry.content:
Hi,

Addition to P&L by Expense we have a requirement to report P&L by Function (GOGS, R&D, Sales/Admin, etc.).
For this purpose in "Manage Global Hierarchies" we have Functional Area based Hierarchy (FAs can be linked to Ledger Accounts or Cost Centers).
To ease up reporting we have been implementing Group Financial Statements Review booklet that has 5 sub pages.
One of the sub pages is "Profit & Loss (Function of Expense)".
Idea was to use that FA hierarchy on that booklet subpage, but that seems not to be possible!
So question is that has someone managed to report FA P&L using Group Financial Statements Review booklet?
If yes can you pls. explain how where you able to do that?

thanks,
Tomi

entry.title: Re: How to do background scheduling with clean core, including Fiori apps, replacing se38 reports
entry.content:
Hi @gurmukh_a 

If you want foreground capabilities, you build a Fiori app for that purpose using RAP, to be clean core compliant. And if you think that means double work, think again. If you build your RAP BO proper, you use the RAP BO in your application job (not vice-versa), with maybe some extra logic, just as in my example for reordering books for my RAP BO. But we definitely don't want any transaction or se38 report, the point was to get away from that and be modern. Hope that helps.

BR,

Melanie

entry.title: In EWM, PRDO -> Goods Issue in PRDO, getting err - User status GIBL is active (8005629903397416)
entry.content:
In EWM, Completed picking/packing/loading.

When trying to do goods Issue in SCWM/PRDO, a pop will come with error as - User status GIBL is active (8005629903397416)

Its not allowing for Goods Issue, Even tried to change HU but its showing its status can not be changed.

- in MON, HU user status is showing as Goods Issue Blocked.

Please guide and suggest, Thank you!

entry.title: Re: ABAP ADT parallel locking issue for ABAP Classes
entry.content:
Dear ADT Experts,

I also have similar issues regarding the object lock in the long term.
For example, only method a of CLASS cl_A  is saved into TR1 in SAP GUI, then if I'd like to edit cl_A's other method b, I cannot type anything and popup shows, telling me the whole class is now locked, and I cannot use ADT to edit method b until the TR1 is released.

Could you please enhance this behavior, as it may happen again if multiple fixing/developing happen on the same class, and it also make me go back to SAP GUI again.

Best regards,
Henry Xu

entry.title: Re: How to do background scheduling with clean core, including Fiori apps, replacing se38 reports
entry.content:
Hello Melaine,

Do we still have the capabilities to schedule the application jobs using the external job scheduling tool like Redwood ?

and if yes what will be the significance/scope that cannot be achieved through normal SAP Application Jobs.

Thanks,

Chetan.

entry.title: Re: How I Built a Raised Bed with Free Barn Wood
entry.content:
Cool idea and great job, @Marian_Zeis!

I can definitely relate to the pride that comes from building something with your own hands, even if your skills are limited.😄

Last year, I renovated the playhouse in our garden and turned it into a hobby horse stable. I removed the sandbox to add a second stable, built and integrated a (fully functioning!) door, completely rebuilt the bridge, waterproofed the ceilings, installed acrylic glass in the windows and surrounding the upper stage (not yet visible in the pictures), sanded everything down, and painted it in my daughter’s favorite color. Took me quite some time, but I learned a lot!

I don’t grow anything in there, but every time I see my daughter and her friends playing in the garden, I feel a little proud. 😊

entry.title: SAP RAP: Automatically add row to table when another entity is created.
entry.content:
Hello,
I have a question regarding SAP RAP development.

I have a case like: one root entity and two children entities A and B with cardinality [0..*]. I use managed RAP scenario with draft and I want to create automatically entity B in draft when the entity A in draft is created. Is it possible somehow?
So it should look like: I have object page of root entity, there are two tables of entities A and B. When I click create on the table with entity A - row to the table with entity B should also be added.
How to achieve that?

Thanks in advance,
Mikolaj

entry.title: Re: Inbound delivery released after packing not appearing in EWM

entry.title: Re: Inbound delivery released after packing not appearing in EWM
entry.content: activated log

entry.title: Re: SAP RAP: Automatically add row to table when another...
entry.content:
Please check "Factory Action" , it should help in such instances.

Thanks,

Sijin

entry.title: Re: the APP mass change of sales order can't set delivery...
entry.content: Yes, but my question is if you tried to do it in another app to check if the same error message appears.

entry.title: Re: Unboxing: Application Backends in SAP Build Apps (Entities)
entry.content: @erza I have no information on that and I do not even see it in the official roadmap. I will check.

entry.title: Re: How to add URL parameters to a function import call i...
entry.content: With actions you can completely control the OData URL parameters, which means you can make use of alot of great OData functionality, whether v2 or v4 ... great that everything is working for you

entry.title: Re: How to add email recipients to EWA
entry.content: Hi Team, My concern is related to the EWA reports which are generated for S4 HANA systems hosted in RISE with SAP landscape. the community link you had shared is responded with the reference to the landscape where SAP Solution Manager is present. Kindly suggest, The SAP S4 HANA systems are hosted in the private cloud of RISE with SAP platform. As such there are no presence of the SAP Solution Manager component in our landscape. I understand the EWA reports are generated only in the url - https://me.sap.com/app/ewaworkspace I want to schedule the EWA report delivery from above mentioned portal to multiple recipient email inbox. I wish you can understand the scenario and help me get the requirement fulfilled.

entry.title: How to extract the total worked hours per employee in SAP HCM
entry.content:
Hello,

We need to extract a report which consist of total worked hours per employees in a plant in SAP HCM .Kindly help us to extract the report.

entry.title: Missing index file at DART/GDPDU extract
entry.content:
Dear Community,

I have introduced the 5XU Scope item for document reporting and compliance at our customer in the public cloud and can therefore also make the Dart extract in the app run statutory reports. we have now handed this over to the customer and he has given it to the auditor to check whether it can be accepted. Here the comment came up that the index file is missing, which the auditor still needs in any case.

Is there a way to activate this by default in the public cloud so that it is also spit out during generation?

Many thanks and best regards,
Vanessa

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Finance

entry.title: Re: the APP mass change of sales order can't set delivery...
entry.content: @Stanislaw_Stefanowski, in the app "Change sales order" , I can change it .

entry.title: Unable to create contact in Fiori App Manage Customer Master Data
entry.content:
Hello,

in Fiori App Manage Customer Master Data, button create doesn't appear.

I have all the autorization mentionned in Fiori App IMPLEMENTATION INFORMATION (Found nothing missing in SU53).

Also I activated oData Service MD_CUSTOMER_MASTER_SRV_01.

I can maintened my Partner, I can even edit or delete a contact, but not create a new one.

We could before the system upgrade.

Do you know what I could be missing ?

Thanks in advance,

Best regards.
